question: Why do the two selection indices disagree most on G1?
good_answer: The transparent and Smith-Hazel indices disagree most on G1 because yield and brix are negatively genetically correlated here (-0.74): G1 ranks high on yield but the genetically-aware index discounts it for the brix it tends to give up. G1 moves 2 ranks between the two views.
bad_answer: The indices disagree on G1 because yield and brix are negatively correlated (-0.31), and G1's heritability of 0.55 makes its ranking unstable.
